mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for equity valuations, directly compressing forward S&P 500 returns over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: Forward 20-day returns show a positive correlation with FEDFUNDS changes over a 3-year rolling window, or Rank IC exceeds 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly unemployment rate increase exceeding 0.3 percentage points signals sudden labor market deterioration, triggering immediate risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 returns within one week.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 5-day returns in more than 55% of instances where UNRATE rises above the 0.3pp threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real GDP and retail sales indicates robust consumer demand, which sustains corporate earnings growth and drives positive S&P 500 returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: The joint positive signal yields negative 60-day forward returns in over 60% of observations, or the interaction term loses statistical significance in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production during a monetary tightening cycle reflects constrained output capacity, which suppresses equity valuations and generates negative S&P 500 returns over one month.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines during tightening periods are followed by positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50% of cases, or the signal shows no predictive power outside the specified regime.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate crossing above 5.0% signals restrictive monetary policy that immediately compresses equity multiples, producing negative S&P 500 returns over a one-week horizon.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 5-day returns remain positive in over 65% of periods where FEDFUNDS exceeds 5.0%, or the threshold effect vanishes in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total employment paired with a falling unemployment rate confirms a strengthening labor market, which supports wage growth and consumer spending, driving positive S&P 500 returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: The combined labor signal fails to predict positive 60-day returns, with a negative correlation coefficient exceeding -0.10 over a 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Higher housing starts indicate increased construction activity and durable goods demand, which directly boosts corporate revenues and generates positive S&P 500 returns over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: HOUST increases are followed by negative 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 55% of observations, or the Rank IC remains below 0.02 across all tested periods.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp monthly decline in industrial production during a post-shock recovery phase signals supply chain fragility, triggering immediate market sell-offs and negative S&P 500 returns within one week.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 5-day returns in over 60% of instances following a >1.0% monthly INDPRO decline, or the signal shows no statistical edge in the specified regime.
